# STEADYWRK — Complete Platform Documentation
> Jordan's AI-native career platform. Where ambition compounds.
> If you are disciplined, bright, and hungry, this is where your curve bends upward.

## Company Overview

STEADYWRK LLC is a US-incorporated, Jordan-operated AI-native career platform. We connect ambitious Jordanian talent — especially women aged 18–30 — with elite training, rapid professional development, and meaningful work serving US clients. We operate four verticals: AI Lab & Studio, Facility Management, Digital Marketing, and AI BPO.

- **Legal Name:** STEADYWRK LLC
- **Headquarters:** Building 15, King Hussein Business Park (KHBP), Amman, Jordan
- **Country of Incorporation:** United States
- **Founded:** 2026
- **Website:** https://steadywrk.app
- **Email:** hello@steadywrk.app
- **Instagram:** @steadywrk
- **LinkedIn:** /company/steadywrk
- **Industry:** AI career platform, BPO, digital marketing, facility management
- **Tagline:** "Where ambition compounds."
- **Application Tagline:** "Apply. Train. Work. Steady."

## The Problem We Solve

Jordan produces over 7,000 technology graduates annually. Despite this talent pipeline, 46% of youth (ages 15–24) face unemployment, with the rate reaching 66% for young women. The pipeline between education and elite work is fundamentally broken. STEADYWRK is building the bridge — connecting Jordan's sharpest minds with US clients through AI-first operations, structured training programs, and a hiring process designed for speed and fairness.

## Company Story

STEADYWRK was born from a simple observation: Jordan has extraordinary talent trapped by a broken employment pipeline. The country ranks 3rd in the Arab world for AI adoption in the public sector. Internet penetration stands at 92.5%, primarily mobile. King Hussein Business Park — our home — is at 100% occupancy with Phase Two expansion underway. The infrastructure is there. The talent is there. What was missing was a platform that operates at the intersection of AI capability and human ambition.

We are not an outsourcing shop. We are an operating company. Every service line is AI-first. Every team member ships production work from day one. Our hiring process targets 14 days from application to offer, and we respond to every applicant within 48 hours. No ghosting. No vague timelines. No "culture fit" guessing.

## Office & Location

STEADYWRK operates from Building 15, King Hussein Business Park (KHBP), Amman, Jordan. KHBP is Jordan's premier business district, housing major technology companies, government entities, and international organizations. The park is at 100% occupancy, with Phase Two expansion underway to meet growing demand. Our location provides:

- Strategic timezone positioning between US East Coast and Asian markets
- Direct access to Jordan's top university graduates
- Modern infrastructure with high-speed connectivity
- Proximity to Queen Alia International Airport (30 minutes)
- Central Amman location accessible by public transport

## Services — Four Verticals, One Platform

### 1. AI Lab & Studio
Research, development, and deployment of AI agent systems. From custom LLM integrations to autonomous workflows — we build the intelligence layer that powers modern operations.

**Capabilities:**
- Custom AI agent development
- LLM integration & fine-tuning
- Autonomous workflow design
- MCP (Model Context Protocol) server architecture
- Prompt engineering and RAG systems
- Tool-use pattern implementation

### 2. Facility Management
End-to-end field service operations. Dispatch coordination, subcontractor management, and maintenance execution across commercial and residential properties.

**Capabilities:**
- Dispatch coordination
- Preventive maintenance programs
- Vendor management
- 24/7 emergency response
- Commercial and residential property servicing
- Subcontractor relationship management

### 3. Digital Marketing
Data-driven campaigns, SEO, content strategy, and brand development. We combine AI tooling with human creativity to deliver measurable growth.

**Capabilities:**
- SEO & content strategy — SEO that ranks
- Paid media management — campaigns that convert
- Brand development — content that compounds
- Analytics & reporting
- Social media management
- Performance marketing

### 4. AI BPO (Business Process Outsourcing)
AI-powered business process outsourcing. Customer support, data processing, document handling, and back-office operations enhanced by intelligent automation.

**Capabilities:**
- AI-enhanced customer support
- Document processing & extraction
- Data entry & validation
- Back-office automation at scale
- Routine inquiry handling via AI systems
- Human-agent focus on judgment calls, escalations, and relationship management

**Our AI BPO Philosophy:** Traditional BPO is a volume game — more agents, more hours, more seats. AI BPO flips this model. Instead of scaling headcount, you scale intelligence. Our BPO agents work alongside AI systems that handle document processing, data extraction, and routine customer inquiries. The human agent focuses on judgment calls, escalations, and relationship management. The result: higher quality output, faster turnaround, and lower cost per transaction. We are not replacing people — we are giving them superpowers.

## Open Positions — Now Hiring in Amman

All positions are based in Amman, Jordan. We are actively building Cohort #1.

### AI Engineer
- **Department:** AI Lab
- **Type:** Full-time
- **Location:** Amman / Remote
- **Salary Range:** 800–1,500 JOD/month
- **Description:** Design and implement AI agent systems, LLM integrations, and autonomous workflows. Experience with prompt engineering, RAG, and tool-use patterns required. You will ship production AI systems in week one.

### Frontend Developer
- **Department:** Engineering
- **Type:** Full-time
- **Location:** Amman / Remote
- **Salary Range:** 700–1,200 JOD/month
- **Description:** Build performant, accessible web interfaces using Next.js, React, and TypeScript. You will work on public-facing platforms and internal tooling. Production deployments from day one.

### Digital Marketing Lead
- **Department:** Growth
- **Type:** Full-time
- **Location:** Amman
- **Salary Range:** 600–1,000 JOD/month
- **Description:** Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across paid and organic channels. Strong analytical skills and experience with SEO, SEM, and social media required. Data-driven approach essential.

### BPO Operations Manager
- **Department:** Operations
- **Type:** Full-time
- **Location:** Amman
- **Salary Range:** 500–900 JOD/month
- **Description:** Coordinate field service operations, manage subcontractor relationships, and ensure service delivery standards across commercial properties. Operational excellence mindset required.

### AI BPO Agent
- **Department:** BPO
- **Type:** Full-time
- **Location:** Amman
- **Salary Range:** 350–500 JOD/month
- **Description:** Handle customer interactions and business processes using AI-enhanced tooling. Training provided. Strong communication skills and attention to detail required.

**How to Apply:** Via WhatsApp or email hello@steadywrk.app. Application takes 6–8 minutes. No account required. We review within 48 hours and respond to every applicant.

## Hiring Pipeline — Our Selection Process

STEADYWRK's hiring pipeline is designed for speed, fairness, and transparency. Our target is 14 days from application to offer.

### Stage 1: Application (Day 1)
Submit your application via WhatsApp or email. Takes 6–8 minutes. No account creation required. We collect basic information, relevant experience, and motivation.

### Stage 2: Initial Review (Days 1–2)
Every application is reviewed by a human within 48 hours. We respond to every single applicant — no ghosting, no black holes. We use structured evaluation criteria, not subjective "culture fit" assessments.

### Stage 3: Structured Scorecard Assessment (Days 3–5)
Qualified candidates proceed to a structured assessment. Same questions for every candidate in the same role. Transparent criteria. Scored against a standardized rubric to eliminate bias. We evaluate technical skills, communication ability, and problem-solving approach.

### Stage 4: Technical or Practical Challenge (Days 5–8)
Role-specific evaluation. Engineers complete a focused coding challenge. Marketers present a campaign strategy. Operations candidates solve a logistics scenario. All challenges are designed to take 2–4 hours maximum.

### Stage 5: Final Interview & Offer (Days 8–14)
Meet the team. Discuss the role, expectations, compensation, and growth trajectory. Offers are made within 14 days of initial application. Compensation is transparent from the first conversation.

### Our Hiring Values
- **Elite Standards:** Hard to get into. Every hire is deliberate. Structured scorecards, transparent criteria.
- **Visible Growth:** Promotions, mentorship, shipped work. Your trajectory is legible — not a vague promise.
- **Real Fairness:** No "culture fit" guessing. Same questions for every candidate. We respond within 48 hours. Always.
- **Speed as Brand:** Fast hiring. Fast feedback. Fast growth. 14-day target. The name promises momentum.

## Career Programs — Three Tracks, One Trajectory

Every program builds real skills with real projects. No simulations. You ship production work serving US clients.

### IGNITE — 3-Month Internship
**Type:** Immersive AI-native internship
**Duration:** 3 months
**What You Do:** Ship production work from week one. Real AI projects, real deadlines, real mentorship. You are not fetching coffee or watching tutorials — you are contributing to live systems used by paying clients. Paired with a senior mentor. Weekly check-ins. Clear milestones.

### ORBIT — 6-Month Fellowship
**Type:** Deep technical fellowship
**Duration:** 6 months
**What You Do:** Lead projects. Mentor juniors. Build the systems that power our operations. ORBIT fellows take ownership of entire workstreams. You architect solutions, review code, and drive technical decisions. This is where specialists become leaders.

### APEX — 12-Month Leadership Track
**Type:** Strategic leadership program
**Duration:** 12 months
**What You Do:** Architect solutions. Drive teams. Shape the company's technical direction. APEX participants operate at the intersection of strategy and execution. You define roadmaps, manage cross-functional teams, and build the organizational systems that scale. This track prepares you for director-level roles.

## Technology Stack

STEADYWRK is built on a modern, AI-first technology stack:

- **Framework:** Next.js 16 (App Router)
- **UI Library:** React 19
- **Language:** TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Styling:** Tailwind CSS v4
- **Animation:** Framer Motion 12
- **Database:** Supabase (Postgres + Auth + Edge Functions + Realtime)
- **Hosting:** Vercel (web applications)
- **Edge Computing:** Cloudflare Workers (MCP servers, edge functions)
- **AI APIs:** Claude API (Anthropic), OpenAI
- **Database (secondary):** Neon Postgres
- **ORM:** Drizzle ORM
- **Analytics:** PostHog
- **Email:** Resend
- **Scheduling:** Cal.com
- **Authentication:** Clerk Auth
- **Payments:** Stripe

### Architecture Principles
- TypeScript strict mode. No `any` types. No implicit returns on complex functions.
- Error handling, rate limiting, and input validation on all API routes.
- Every feature gets a test. QA enforced.
- Conventional commits: feat:, fix:, chore:, docs:
- No console.log in production. Structured logging only.
- Supabase Row Level Security (RLS) on every table. No exceptions.
- No secrets in code. Environment variables only.
- Mobile-first design. 92.5% of Jordan is online on phones. If it fails on phone, it fails.

## 2026 Roadmap

### Q1 2026 — Launch
- Platform live at steadywrk.app
- First hires in Jordan onboarded
- Core services operational across all four verticals

- AI Lab delivers first client projects
- BPO team fully onboarded and operational
- Client acquisition pipeline active and converting

### Q3 2026 — Expand
- US operations activated
- Facility management contracts signed
- Marketing client portfolio growing month-over-month

### Q4 2026 — Steady
- Full service delivery across all four verticals at scale
- MCP platform layer launched as differentiator
- Series readiness achieved

## Blog

### SteadyWrk Has Landed in Jordan
**Published:** March 21, 2026

SteadyWrk is officially operational in Jordan. We are a US-based company building a multi-vertical platform that spans AI development, facility management, digital marketing, and AI-powered BPO services.

Jordan offers a unique combination: a highly educated workforce, strategic timezone positioning between US and Asian markets, and a growing tech ecosystem. We are here to build, hire, and deliver.

Our first wave of hiring is open. If you are a developer, marketer, operations coordinator, or AI enthusiast — we want to hear from you.

### The Future of AI-Powered BPO
**Published:** March 18, 2026

Traditional BPO is a volume game — more agents, more hours, more seats. AI BPO flips this model. Instead of scaling headcount, you scale intelligence.

At SteadyWrk, our BPO agents work alongside AI systems that handle document processing, data extraction, and routine customer inquiries. The human agent focuses on judgment calls, escalations, and relationship management.

The result: higher quality output, faster turnaround, and lower cost per transaction. We are not replacing people — we are giving them superpowers.

## Key Market Facts

- Jordan produces approximately 7,000 technology graduates annually
- 46% youth unemployment rate (ages 15–24)
- 66% unemployment rate for young women in the same age bracket
- Jordan ranks 3rd in the Arab world for AI adoption in the public sector
- 92.5% internet penetration, primarily mobile
- King Hussein Business Park is at 100% occupancy with Phase Two expansion underway
- Strategic timezone: GMT+3, bridging US Eastern (6–8 hours ahead) and East Asian markets

## Brand Identity

- **Name Format:** STEADYWRK (all caps in logo and headers), "Steadywrk" in body text. No vowel in "Wrk" — intentional.
- **Primary Color:** Signal Amber #E58A0F
- **Hover Color:** #CC7408
- **Background:** #FAFAF8 (warm white)
- **Text Color:** #23211D (near-black)
- **Secondary Text:** #6E695F
- **Fonts:** Cabinet Grotesk (display/headings), Satoshi (body text) — both via Fontshare
- **Design Philosophy:** 90% neutral, amber is the reward. Maximum 3 amber elements per viewport. Mobile-first. Every transition 180ms ease-out.

## Domain Architecture

- **steadywrk.app** — Public funnel. Careers, brand, roles, proof. The talent honeypot.
- **steadywrk.dev** — Operations portal. Employee dashboard, dispatch, agent orchestration.

## SEO & Structured Data

STEADYWRK implements schema.org/Organization markup, provides /llms.txt and /llms-full.txt for LLM consumption, maintains a complete sitemap.xml, and follows robots.txt best practices for search engine optimization. All pages are server-rendered with Next.js for optimal crawlability.

## Why Jordan — The Strategic Case

Jordan represents one of the most compelling talent arbitrage opportunities in the global technology market. The country's education system produces a steady pipeline of 7,000+ ICT graduates annually from institutions including the University of Jordan, Jordan University of Science and Technology (JUST), Princess Sumaya University for Technology (PSUT), and the German Jordanian University. English proficiency is widespread among university graduates, with most programs taught in English. Jordan's regulatory environment is business-friendly, with free zone benefits, investment incentives, and a stable legal framework modeled on common law principles.

**Timezone Advantage:** Jordan operates at GMT+3, positioning it ideally to serve both US and European clients. When New York opens at 9:00 AM EST, it is 4:00 PM in Amman — allowing a full overlap window for synchronous collaboration. For asynchronous workflows, the timezone offset enables true 24-hour productivity cycles.

**Cost Efficiency:** Jordan offers significant cost advantages compared to US-based hiring without the quality compromises typically associated with outsourcing. A senior developer in Amman costs 60–75% less than a comparable hire in a US tier-2 city, while delivering equivalent technical output. STEADYWRK passes these efficiencies to clients while paying above-market rates locally.

**Cultural Compatibility:** Jordan has deep cultural ties with the United States. Over 100,000 Jordanian-Americans maintain active connections between both countries. Jordanian professionals are accustomed to working with American clients and understand US business culture, communication norms, and quality expectations.

**Infrastructure:** Amman has robust internet infrastructure with multiple fiber providers offering symmetric gigabit connections. King Hussein Business Park provides enterprise-grade connectivity, backup power systems, and physical security. Jordan's telecommunications infrastructure ranks among the most developed in the MENA region.

## The MCP Platform Layer — Our Technical Differentiator

STEADYWRK's AI Lab is building a proprietary MCP (Model Context Protocol) platform layer deployed on Cloudflare Workers at the edge. MCP is the emerging standard for connecting AI models to external tools and data sources. Our MCP servers enable:

- **Custom Tool Integration:** Any business process, API, or data source can be exposed as an MCP tool that AI agents can invoke autonomously. This means AI agents can check inventory, process orders, update CRMs, or trigger workflows without human intervention.
- **Multi-Model Architecture:** Our MCP servers are model-agnostic. They work with Claude (Anthropic), GPT (OpenAI), Gemini (Google), and open-source models. Clients are not locked into a single AI provider.
- **Edge Deployment:** Running on Cloudflare Workers means sub-50ms latency globally. MCP tools execute at the edge, close to where they are needed. No cold starts. No centralized bottleneck.
- **Composable Workflows:** MCP tools can be chained, parallelized, and orchestrated. A single AI agent can coordinate across dozens of tools to complete complex multi-step workflows — from ingesting a document to extracting data to updating a database to sending a notification.

This platform layer is what separates STEADYWRK from traditional BPO providers. We are not just providing labor — we are providing intelligent infrastructure that makes every human operator dramatically more productive.

## Employee Benefits & Work Environment

STEADYWRK provides a modern, employee-first work environment:

- **Competitive Compensation:** Above-market salaries for Jordan, paid consistently on the 1st of each month. Salary ranges are transparent from the first conversation. No negotiation games.
- **Health Coverage:** Comprehensive health insurance for all full-time employees.
- **Professional Development:** Access to AI tools, learning resources, conference attendance, and certification support. Every team member gets a personal development budget.
- **Equipment:** Company-provided laptops and peripherals for all roles. Engineering team gets Apple Silicon machines. Design team gets high-resolution displays.
- **Flexible Work:** Hybrid model available for most roles. Core hours ensure overlap with US clients. Remote-first for engineering positions.
- **Modern Office:** Building 15 at KHBP features open workspaces, meeting rooms, a prayer room, and a stocked kitchen. High-speed fiber internet with redundant connections.
- **Growth Trajectory:** Clear promotion paths documented and shared. No ambiguity about what it takes to advance. Quarterly performance conversations, not annual reviews.

## Client Engagement Model

STEADYWRK serves US-based clients across all four verticals. Our engagement models include:

- **Dedicated Teams:** A full team assigned exclusively to one client. Operates as an extension of the client's organization. Best for ongoing, long-term engagements.
- **Project-Based:** Scoped deliverables with defined timelines and milestones. Best for specific initiatives like building an AI agent system, launching a marketing campaign, or setting up facility management operations.
- **Staff Augmentation:** Individual specialists embedded in client teams. They attend standups, use client tools, and operate under client management. Best for filling specific skill gaps.
- **Managed Services:** STEADYWRK owns the outcome. We define processes, manage quality, and deliver results. Clients define what they need; we figure out how. Best for BPO and facility management engagements.

All engagements include structured onboarding, dedicated account management, and regular performance reporting.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is STEADYWRK a staffing agency?**
A: No. STEADYWRK is an operating company. We hire full-time employees, train them with our programs (IGNITE, ORBIT, APEX), and deploy them on client work. We own the quality and the process. Our people are our team — not contractors placed at client sites.

**Q: What makes STEADYWRK different from other BPO companies in Jordan?**
A: Three things. First, we are AI-native — every process is designed with AI augmentation from the start, not bolted on. Second, we run four verticals (AI Lab, Facility Management, Digital Marketing, AI BPO), which means we offer breadth no single-vertical shop can match. Third, our MCP platform layer gives our operators intelligent tooling that multiplies their output.

**Q: Do you hire internationally?**
A: Our operations are based in Amman, Jordan. Most roles require presence in Jordan, though select engineering and AI positions are open to remote candidates in compatible timezones (GMT+1 to GMT+5). We sponsor work permits for exceptional international candidates relocating to Jordan.

**Q: What is the interview process like?**
A: Five stages over 14 days: Application (Day 1) → Initial Review (Days 1-2) → Structured Scorecard (Days 3-5) → Technical Challenge (Days 5-8) → Final Interview & Offer (Days 8-14). Same process for every candidate. No surprise stages. We respond to every applicant within 48 hours.

**Q: What technologies do your engineers work with?**
A: Next.js 16, React 19, TypeScript (strict), Tailwind CSS v4, Supabase, Vercel, Cloudflare Workers, Claude API, OpenAI, Drizzle ORM, PostHog, and Framer Motion. We use cutting-edge tools and ship to production daily.

**Q: What are the salary ranges?**
A: We publish salary ranges for every role. AI Engineer: 800–1,500 JOD/month. Frontend Developer: 700–1,200 JOD/month. Digital Marketing Lead: 600–1,000 JOD/month. BPO Operations Manager: 500–900 JOD/month. AI BPO Agent: 350–500 JOD/month. All salaries are above Jordan market rates.

**Q: How do I apply?**
A: Via WhatsApp (primary) or email hello@steadywrk.app. The application takes 6–8 minutes. No account creation required. We review every application within 48 hours.

## Compliance & Legal

- **Legal Entity:** STEADYWRK LLC, registered in the United States
- **Jordan Operations:** Fully licensed for business operations in the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan
- **Data Protection:** We follow industry-standard data protection practices. Client data is processed in accordance with applicable US and Jordanian data protection regulations.
- **Employment Law:** All Jordan-based employees are hired in compliance with Jordanian labor law, including social security contributions, annual leave entitlements, and overtime regulations.
